description: Add tenant_feature_overrides schema table + updated module gate system + user-directory API + feature-control API + announcements/email-blast API. This is the backend foundation for all super admin granular control features.
steps: 1. Create new schema file `drizzle/schema/feature-overrides.ts` with a `tenantFeatureOverrides` table: id (pk), tenantId (uuid FK tenants), featureKey (text, e.g. 'telegram', 'email-sequences', 'ai-assistant'), enabled (boolean), grantedBy (uuid FK users), grantedAt (timestamp), reason (text nullable), metadata (jsonb). Add unique index on (tenantId, featureKey). Use utils.pk(), utils.lifecycle().
2. Export the new table from `drizzle/schema/index.ts` by adding `export * from './feature-overrides';`.
3. Register the new table in `drizzle/schema/_registry.ts` - add import and registry entry following existing patterns.

4. Update `lib/modules/gate.ts` - add a new function `checkFeatureOverride(tenantId: string, featureKey: string)` that queries `tenantFeatureOverrides` to check if super admin has explicitly enabled/disabled a feature for a tenant. Modify `requireModule()` to also check this table before the plan-gate. If an override with enabled=false exists, block access even if plan allows it. If enabled=true, allow access even if plan blocks it.

5. Create API route `app/api/superadmin/user-directory/route.ts` with GET handler that returns ALL users across ALL tenants with: id, email, fullName, phone, lastTenantId, emailVerified, isSuperAdmin, createdAt, and their tenant memberships with tenant name/plan/status. Support query params: ?q (search email/name/phone), ?tenant_id (filter by tenant), ?page and ?limit for pagination. Follow the existing auth pattern from app/api/superadmin/users/route.ts.

6. Create API route `app/api/superadmin/feature-control/route.ts` with: GET - list all tenants with their feature overrides (join tenants + tenantFeatureOverrides), PATCH - create/update a feature override for a tenant (body: { tenant_id, feature_key, enabled, reason? }). Use zod validation.

7. Create API route `app/api/superadmin/email-blast/route.ts` with POST handler that accepts { subject, html_body, text_body?, target: 'all' | 'tenant' | 'plan', target_value?: string (tenant_id or plan slug), filter_verified_only?: boolean }. It should query the target users, then call sendEmail from lib/email/service.ts for each (batch with Promise.allSettled in chunks of 10). Return { sent: number, failed: number }. Require super admin auth.

8. Add a predefined list of controllable features as a constant in a new file `lib/modules/feature-keys.ts`: CONTROLLABLE_FEATURES array with objects { key: string, label: string, description: string, category: string }. Include: 'telegram', 'email-sequences', 'ai-assistant', 'whatsapp', 'lead-warming', 'analytics-pro', 'automation-pro', 'forms-builder', 'compliance-suite'. The feature-control API should reference this list.
acceptance_criteria: New schema file drizzle/schema/feature-overrides.ts exports tenantFeatureOverrides table
lib/modules/gate.ts requireModule() checks tenantFeatureOverrides before plan-gate
GET /api/superadmin/user-directory returns users with memberships, supports ?q search and ?tenant_id filter
GET /api/superadmin/feature-control returns tenants with their overrides
POST /api/superadmin/email-blast sends emails to filtered user groups
All new API routes require super admin authentication
npm run build passes without TypeScript errors
verification: Run `npm run build` and confirm zero TypeScript errors
Run `npm test` and confirm existing tests still pass
findings: Build and all 435 tests pass. The gate.ts now uses isNull for deletedAt filtering following the codebase pattern. The drizzle query builder schema is properly registered with relational query support (db.query.tenantFeatureOverrides).
